items.title: investingLive Americas FX news wrap 18 Jun USDJPY surges to near highs going to 2024
items.summary: Russell 2000 rises by 2.12% and NASDAQ by 1.91% to lead stocks higher Trump: US is committed to PEACE Iran supreme leader responds to US MOU with caution Bitcoin price action is spelling T-R-O-U-B-L-E for the buyers US list blockade of maritime traffic entering and exiting the Straits of Hormuz Key Points from the MOU between the US and Iran. Major European indices close mixed with a bigger tilt to the upside VP Vance: We will manage flare-ups through diplomacy. Trump: Iran not a threat. Oil lower. Stocks higher. Prices dropping. Bloomberg reports that Kuwait is boosting oil output. Crude oil tests key 200 day MA US June Philly Fed business index 10.3 versus 10.0 estimate US initial jobless claims 226K vs 225K estimate. Continuing Claims 1.810M vs 1.795M est Canada May PPI +1.2% m/m vs +1.8% expected ECBs Lane: Hikes aim to contain spread of energy shock investingLive European FX news wrap: SNB and BoE steady; US dollar extends gains The USD continued its run to the upside after the more hawkish Fed yesterday. The gains in the greenback were led by gains versus the GBP (+0.68%), the CHF (+0.56%) and the JPY (+0.47%). The BOE and the SNB both kept rates unchanged. For the Bank of England, although the skew is toward higher rates, the central bank seems to be on hold for the foreseeable future as risks of slower growth balances things out a bit versus inflation. For the SNB, they indicated that intervention is not out of the question against the strong CHF. The USDJPY started to step higher after breaking above the 2026 high at 160.717 and squeezed higher as the price continued away from the 160.00 level and move to and through 161.00 instead. Anxiety about intervention reared its ugly head once the price extended up to 161.81 just about 11 or 12 pips short of the highest level going back to 2024. The price quickly move down to a low of 160.887 but then bounced back higher and is trading near 161.40 heading into the end of day. Traders can expect more anxiety going forward, but the buyers are still more in control as long at the old high for the year at 160.717 holds support. Move below it and staying below is now needed to give the sellers some control from a technical perspective back. US stocks after falling yesterday, recovered nicely today help by the chip stocks. The trading week for stocks ends today due to the Juneteenth holiday tomorrow. For the week, the major indices closed higher: Dow industrial average +0.14%. S&P index +1.08% NASDAQ index +1.91% Russell 2000 + 2.12% The US debt market will also be closed tomorrow. Yields in the short end edged higher today, while the yields in the longer end fell as the yield flattening continues. 2 year yield 4.1809%, +1.8 basis points 5 year 4.234%, +5.2 basis points 10 year 4.457%, -5.8 basis points 30 year 4.899%, -2.7 basis points For the week, the 2 year yield is up around 10.0 basis points. 10 year yield is down -2.6 basis points. Fundamentally: US initial jobless claims came in as expected at 226K vs 225K estimate Philly Fed regional index was also near expectation at 10.3 vs 10.0 estimate. . The U.S.-Iran Memorandum of Understanding was released/leaked. It showed a 14-point framework designed to end hostilities, reopen trade and energy flows, and provide a path toward a broader peace and nuclear agreement. The deal calls for an immediate halt to military operations, a reopening of the Strait of Hormuz with safe commercial passage, and the removal of the U.S. naval blockade. Both sides have agreed to negotiate a final agreement within 60 days. Iran reaffirmed that it will not develop nuclear weapons and will work with international inspectors on its nuclear program, while the U.S. pledged sanctions relief, access to frozen Iranian assets, and support for a reconstruction and economic development package worth at least $300 billion. The agreement also allows Iranian oil exports to resume, establishes a monitoring mechanism for compliance, and envisions a final deal that would be backed by a binding U.N. Security Council resolution. Markets have viewed the agreement as a major de-escalation event, helping ease concerns over oil supply disruptions and broader regional instability This article was written by Greg Michalowski at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 罗素2000指数上涨2.12%，纳斯达克指数上涨1.91%，领涨大盘。特朗普：美国致力于和平。伊朗最高领袖对美伊谅解备忘录作出谨慎回应。比特币价格走势对买家而言预示着“麻烦”。美国发布禁令，封锁进出霍尔木兹海峡的海上交通。美伊谅解备忘录要点。欧洲主要股指收盘涨跌不一，整体偏向上涨 副国务卿万斯：我们将通过外交手段管控局势升级。特朗普：伊朗不构成威胁。油价下跌。股市上涨。物价走低。彭博社报道称科威特正在增加原油产量。原油价格测试关键的200日均线 美国6月费城联储商业指数为10.3，高于10.0的预期 美国初请失业金人数为22.6万，高于22.5万的预期。续请失业金人数181万，预期179.5万；加拿大5月生产者物价指数（PPI）环比+1.2%，预期+1.8%；欧洲央行莱恩：加息旨在遏制能源冲击的蔓延；investingLive欧洲外汇新闻综述：瑞士央行和英国央行维持利率不变；美元延续涨势。在美联储昨日释放更强硬信号后，美元延续了上涨势头。美元的上涨主要得益于其对英镑（+0.68%）、瑞士法郎（+0.56%）和日元（+0.47%）的升值。英国央行和瑞士央行均维持利率不变。 就英格兰银行而言，尽管市场预期倾向于加息，但鉴于经济增长放缓的风险在一定程度上抵消了通胀压力，该央行在可预见的未来似乎将维持利率不变。瑞士央行方面则表示，针对瑞士法郎走强，不排除采取干预措施的可能性。美元兑日元在突破2026年高点160.717后开始攀升，随着价格持续远离160.00关口，并进一步上行至161.00并突破该水平，汇价进一步走高。当价格升至161.81（距离2024年以来的最高点仅差11或12点）时，市场对干预的担忧再度浮现。价格迅速回落至160.887的低点，但随后反弹回升，临近收盘时交投于161.40附近。交易者预计未来市场将持续动荡，但只要年内前高160.717仍能提供支撑，多头仍占据主导地位。从技术角度看，卖方若要重新掌握主动权，价格必须跌破该水平并持续维持在下方。美国股市在昨日下跌后，今日在芯片股的带动下强劲反弹。 由于明天是“六月节”（Juneteenth）假期，本周股市交易今日收官。本周主要股指收高：道琼斯工业平均指数上涨0.14%，标普500指数上涨1.08%，纳斯达克指数上涨1.91%，罗素2000指数上涨2.12%。美国债券市场明天也将休市。今日短期债券收益率小幅走高，而长期债券收益率则有所回落，收益率曲线趋平的趋势仍在持续。2年期收益率4.1809%，+1.8个基点；5年期4.234%，+5.2个基点；10年期4.457%，-5.8个基点；30年期4.899%，-2.7个基点。本周，2年期收益率上涨约10.0个基点。10年期收益率下跌2.6个基点。基本面方面：美国初请失业金人数为22.6万，符合预期（市场预估22.5万）；费城联储地区指数为10.3，也接近预期（市场预估10.0）。美伊谅解备忘录已发布/泄露。该文件提出了一项14点框架，旨在结束敌对行动、恢复贸易和能源流动，并为达成更广泛的和平与核协议铺平道路。该协议要求立即停止军事行动，重新开放霍尔木兹海峡以确保商业船只安全通行，并解除美国海军封锁。 双方已同意在60天内就最终协议进行谈判。伊朗重申不会研发核武器，并将就其核计划与国际核查人员合作；而美国则承诺解除制裁、解冻伊朗资产，并支持一项价值至少3000亿美元的重建与经济发展计划。该协议还允许伊朗恢复石油出口，建立了合规监督机制，并设想最终协议将获得具有约束力的联合国安理会决议的支持。市场将该协议视为一次重大的局势缓和事件，有助于缓解对石油供应中断及更广泛地区动荡的担忧。本文作者为investinglive.com的格雷格·米哈洛夫斯基。

items.title: investingLive European FX news wrap: SNB and BoE steady; US dollar extends gains
items.summary: BoE leaves bank rate unchanged at 3.75% in June meeting, as expected The US dollar rises to the highest level since May 2025 on increased Fed rate hike bets Gold closes the gap following the hawkish Fed's dot plot; tightening bias caps the upside SNB's Schlegel dodges question on the addition of "if necessary" about FX intervention SNB leaves key policy rate unchanged at 0% in June meeting, as widely expected Bitcoin price forecast: why the $61,775 level matters now UK April ILO unemployment rate 4.9% vs 5.0% expected FX option expiries for 18 June 10am New York cut What are the main events for today? It's been a pretty calm session in terms of data and news releases. The UK employment report was better than expected with a tick lower in the unemployment rate and stronger wage growth. The data led to some firming in BoE rate hike bets but nothing major. The SNB left the policy rate unchanged at 0.00% as widely expected but added "if necessary" to the line saying "readiness to intervene in forex markets is higher". The Swiss franc was barely changed in the aftermath but started to weaken after SNB Chairman Schlegel avoided to answer the question on why that new line was added. The market interpreted that as "dovish". The BoE left the Bank Rate unchanged at 3.75% as widely expected with no changes to the April's statement. The central bank repeated that MPC stands ready to act as necessary to ensure CPI meets 2% target in the medium term. Coming into the meeting, the market was pricing 35 bps of tightening by year-end with 58% chance of a rate hike in September. That remained the same following the decision. The main highlight of the session was the US dollar. The greenback extended the gains and reached the highest level since May 2025 amid Fed rate hike bets. This article was written by Giuseppe Dellamotta at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 英国央行在6月会议上如预期般将基准利率维持在3.75%不变 受美联储加息预期升温推动，美元升至2025年5月以来的最高水平 受美联储鹰派点阵图影响，黄金价格填补了缺口； 但紧缩倾向限制了上行空间 瑞士央行（SNB）的施莱格尔回避了关于在外汇干预中是否添加“如有必要”这一表述的问题 瑞士央行（SNB）在6月会议上将关键政策利率维持在0%不变，符合市场普遍预期 比特币价格预测： 为何61,775美元关口当前至关重要 英国4月国际劳工组织（ILO）失业率为4.9%，低于预期值5.0% 6月18日纽约时间上午10点外汇期权到期清单 今日有哪些主要事件？就数据和新闻发布而言，今日交易时段相当平静。英国就业报告好于预期，失业率微降，工资增长势头更强。该数据推动市场对英国央行加息的预期有所增强，但影响有限。瑞士央行如市场普遍预期将政策利率维持在0.00%不变，但在“准备在外汇市场进行干预的意愿更高”这一表述中新增了“如有必要”的措辞。瑞士法郎在消息公布后基本持平，但在瑞士央行行长施莱格尔回避了关于为何添加该新表述的问题后，开始走弱。 市场将此解读为“鸽派”信号。英国央行如市场普遍预期般将基准利率维持在3.75%不变，4月份的政策声明内容也未作调整。央行重申，货币政策委员会（MPC）随时准备采取必要行动，以确保消费者物价指数（CPI）在中期内达到2%的目标。在此次会议召开前，市场预期年底前将加息35个基点，9月加息的概率为58%。这一预期在决议公布后保持不变。本次会议的主要焦点在于美元。受美联储加息预期推动，美元延续涨势，升至2025年5月以来的最高水平。本文作者为investinglive.com的朱塞佩·德拉莫塔（Giuseppe Dellamotta）。

It's been a pretty calm session in terms of data and news releases. The UK employment report was better than expected with a tick lower in the unemployment rate and stronger wage growth. The data led to some firming in BoE rate hike bets but nothing major. The SNB left the policy rate unchanged at 0.00% as widely expected but added "if necessary" to the line saying "readiness to intervene in forex markets is higher". The Swiss franc was barely changed in the aftermath but started to weaken after SNB Chairman Schlegel avoided to answer the question on why that new line was added. The market interpreted that as "dovish". The BoE left the Bank Rate unchanged at 3.75% as widely expected with no changes to the April's statement. The central bank repeated that MPC stands ready to act as necessary to ensure CPI meets 2% target in the medium term. The main highlight of the session was the US dollar. The greenback extended the gains and reached the highest level since May 2025 amid Fed rate hike bets. This article was written by Giuseppe Dellamotta at investinglive.com.

items.title: investingLive Asia-Pacific FX news wrap: US-Iran memorandum of understanding signed
items.summary: Warsh leaves markets guessing as Fed framework overhaul raises more questions than answers China rolls out third trade-in fund tranche as weak retail data keeps stimulus pressure on Japan's Kihara flags weak yen household burden but offers no fresh intervention signal Apple warns memory chip crunch makes price rises unavoidable, Cook tells WSJ PBOC sets USD/ CNY reference rate for today at 6.8130 (vs. estimate at 6.7752) Analysts split on Fed path after June hold, with December hike odds near 50% CITIC Securities sees Fed on hold all year as Warsh faces political & inflation crosswinds Brazil central bank cuts rates but warns fiscal stimulus may blunt monetary policy SpaceX stock analysis after IPO Japan's 94% Middle East oil dependence leaves firms deeply exposed even as war winds down Brent could top $130 if strait never fully reopens, Goldman warns New Zealand Q1 GDP 0.8% q/q (0.9% expected) and 1.5% y/y (1.1% expected) Iran draws a red line on the deal. Attack Lebanon and its off. Iran-US MOU signed but Baghaei fires warning on missiles, uranium and Hormuz fees Iran confirms MoU with the US has been agreed to and finalised. Both sides have signed. Warsh rewrites the Fed playbook as FOMC holds rates and signals hikes ahead Iran signals permanent Hormuz changes as $300bn reconstruction deal confirmed US stocks fall as Fed signals a more hawkish policy investingLive Americas market news wrap: Warsh leans heavily into the inflation mandate Summary: US-Iran MOU formally signed: Trump at Versailles, Pezeshkian in Tehran; 14-point agreement now in effect Oil dribbled lower on the signing; gold clawed back some post-Fed losses New Zealand Q1 GDP: 0.8% q/q, 1.5% y/y; NZD/USD crept higher on broad USD softness Japan's Kihara delivered standard verbal intervention; USD/JPY unmoved, holding around 160.50 China NDRC announced third consumer goods trade-in tranche of 62.5 bln yuan by end-June Nikkei 225 surged past 71,000 for the first time; Topix reached 4,069 The headline of the Asia-Pacific session was the formal signing of the US-Iran memorandum of understanding, bringing the framework agreement to end the Middle East conflict into legal effect. President Trump signed his copy of the 14-point document during a dinner at the Palace of Versailles, with a photograph of the signed agreement transmitted to Tehran and the mediating countries. Iranian President Pezeshkian signed separately. Oil prices edged modestly lower on the news, with markets having largely priced the outcome. Gold recovered some ground after Wednesday's hawkish Fed-driven selloff. New Zealand's Q1 GDP delivered a beat on the annual measure at 1.5% against an expected 1.1%, though the quarterly print of 0.8% fell short of the RBNZ's 1.0% forecast. The NZD crept higher but the move was modest, driven more by broad dollar softness across the majors than by the data itself. Japan's chief cabinet secretary Kihara ran through the standard FX watchfulness script, acknowledging the household burden of yen weakness without offering anything that moved the market. USD/JPY held around 160.50. The session's standout market move was in Japanese equities. The Nikkei 225 broke above 71,000 for the first time on record, with the broader Topix reaching 4,069, as the Versailles signing and a softer dollar provided the constructive backdrop. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 美联储政策框架改革引发更多疑问而非解答，沃什的表态令市场捉摸不透；中国推出第三批以旧换新基金，疲软的零售数据持续给刺激政策带来压力；日本木原行长指出日元走弱给家庭带来负担，但未释放新的干预信号；苹果警告称内存芯片短缺导致涨价不可避免，库克向《华尔街日报》透露；中国人民银行将今日美元兑人民币中间价定为6.8130（市场预期为6.7752） 6月按兵不动后，分析师对美联储政策路径意见分歧，12月加息概率接近50% 中信证券预计美联储全年按兵不动，因沃什面临政治与通胀的双重阻力 巴西央行降息，但警告财政刺激可能削弱货币政策效果 SpaceX上市后的股票分析 日本94%的中东石油依赖度使企业面临巨大风险，即便战争趋于平息 若海峡无法完全重开，布伦特原油价格可能突破130美元， 高盛警告称，新西兰第一季度GDP环比增长0.8%（预期0.9%），同比增长1.5%（预期1.1%）；伊朗就协议划出红线。若袭击黎巴嫩，协议将告吹。伊朗与美国签署谅解备忘录，但巴盖伊就导弹、铀及霍尔木兹海峡过境费发出警告；伊朗确认已与美国就谅解备忘录达成一致并最终敲定，双方均已签署。 美联储公开市场委员会（FOMC）维持利率不变并暗示未来将加息，沃什重写美联储政策指南；伊朗暗示将对霍尔木兹海峡实施永久性调整，3000亿美元重建协议获确认；美联储释放更鹰派政策信号，美股下跌；investingLive美洲市场新闻综述：沃什大力强调通胀目标 摘要： 美伊谅解备忘录正式签署：特朗普在凡尔赛宫，佩泽什基安在德黑兰；14点协议现已生效 油价在签署后小幅走低；黄金收复了部分美联储决议后的跌幅 新西兰第一季度GDP：环比增长0.8%，同比增长1.5%；受美元整体走软影响，纽元兑美元小幅走高 日本的木原发表了常规口头干预； 美元兑日元持平，维持在160.50附近；中国国家发展和改革委员会宣布，将于6月底前推出第三批总额625亿元的消费品以旧换新计划；日经225指数首次突破71,000点； 东证指数升至4,069点。亚太交易时段的头条新闻是美伊谅解备忘录的正式签署，这使旨在结束中东冲突的框架协议正式生效。 特朗普总统在凡尔赛宫举行的晚宴上签署了这份14点文件的副本，签署后的协议照片已转交德黑兰及各调解国。伊朗总统佩泽什基安则另行签署。受此消息影响，油价小幅走低，因市场已基本消化了这一结果。在周三受美联储鹰派言论推动的抛售潮后，金价有所回升。新西兰第一季度GDP同比增速达1.5%，高于预期的1.1%，但环比增速0.8%未达新西兰联储1.0%的预测。新西兰元小幅走高，但涨幅有限，这更多是受主要货币对美元普遍走软的推动，而非数据本身所致。日本内阁官房长官木原重信重申了惯常的外汇谨慎表态，承认日元走弱给家庭带来的负担，但未提出任何能影响市场的具体措施。美元兑日元维持在160.50附近。本交易日最引人注目的市场动向出现在日本股市。受《凡尔赛协定》签署及美元走软的积极影响，日经225指数首次突破71,000点大关，更广泛的东证指数（Topix）则升至4,069点。 本文由investinglive.com网站的伊蒙·谢里丹撰写。
items.summaryEn: Warsh leaves markets guessing as Fed framework overhaul raises more questions than answers China rolls out third trade-in fund tranche as weak retail data keeps stimulus pressure on Japan's Kihara flags weak yen household burden but offers no fresh intervention signal Apple warns memory chip crunch makes price rises unavoidable, Cook tells WSJ PBOC sets USD/ CNY reference rate for today at 6.8130 (vs. estimate at 6.7752) Analysts split on Fed path after June hold, with December hike odds near 50% CITIC Securities sees Fed on hold all year as Warsh faces political & inflation crosswinds Brazil central bank cuts rates but warns fiscal stimulus may blunt monetary policy SpaceX stock analysis after IPO Japan's 94% Middle East oil dependence leaves firms deeply exposed even as war winds down Brent could top $130 if strait never fully reopens, Goldman warns New Zealand Q1 GDP 0.8% q/q (0.9% expected) and 1.5% y/y (1.1% expected) Iran draws a red line on the deal. Attack Lebanon and its off. Iran-US MOU signed but Baghaei fires warning on missiles, uranium and Hormuz fees Iran confirms MoU with the US has been agreed to and finalised. Both sides have signed. Warsh rewrites the Fed playbook as FOMC holds rates and signals hikes ahead Iran signals permanent Hormuz changes as $300bn reconstruction deal confirmed US stocks fall as Fed signals a more hawkish policy investingLive Americas market news wrap: Warsh leans heavily into the inflation mandate Summary: US-Iran MOU formally signed: Trump at Versailles, Pezeshkian in Tehran; 14-point agreement now in effect Oil dribbled lower on the signing; gold clawed back some post-Fed losses New Zealand Q1 GDP: 0.8% q/q, 1.5% y/y; NZD/USD crept higher on broad USD softness Japan's Kihara delivered standard verbal intervention; USD/JPY unmoved, holding around 160.50 China NDRC announced third consumer goods trade-in tranche of 62.5 bln yuan by end-June Nikkei 225 surged past 71,000 for the first time; Topix reached 4,069 The headline of the Asia-Pacific session was the formal signing of the US-Iran memorandum of understanding, bringing the framework agreement to end the Middle East conflict into legal effect. President Trump signed his copy of the 14-point document during a dinner at the Palace of Versailles, with a photograph of the signed agreement transmitted to Tehran and the mediating countries. Iranian President Pezeshkian signed separately. Oil prices edged modestly lower on the news, with markets having largely priced the outcome. Gold recovered some ground after Wednesday's hawkish Fed-driven selloff. New Zealand's Q1 GDP delivered a beat on the annual measure at 1.5% against an expected 1.1%, though the quarterly print of 0.8% fell short of the RBNZ's 1.0% forecast. The NZD crept higher but the move was modest, driven more by broad dollar softness across the majors than by the data itself. Japan's chief cabinet secretary Kihara ran through the standard FX watchfulness script, acknowledging the household burden of yen weakness without offering anything that moved the market. USD/JPY held around 160.50. The session's standout market move was in Japanese equities. The Nikkei 225 broke above 71,000 for the first time on record, with the broader Topix reaching 4,069, as the Versailles signing and a softer dollar provided the constructive backdrop. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
